channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Thieves steal paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse from a private museum in Italy
item.description: Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy.
item.content:encoded:
Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy.

(Image credit: Domenico Stinellis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Farmworker communities are still reeling after Chavez sexual abuse allegations
item.description: Cesar Chavez once lived in Delano, home of the United Farm Workers' first HQ. In the wake of sexual abuse allegations against him, many in the city struggle over removing his name from public spaces.
item.content:encoded: Cesar Chavez once lived in Delano, home of the United Farm Workers' first HQ. In the wake of sexual abuse allegations against him, many in the city struggle over removing his name from public spaces.
item.dc:creator: Kerry Klein

item.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
item.description: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.
item.content:encoded:
Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

(Image credit: Michal Cizek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: He's the voice of romantasy audiobooks' biggest heartthrobs. He's never been busier
item.description: From Rhysand in A Court of Thorns and Roses to Kingfisher in "Fae and Alchemy," Anthony Palmini is a voiceover star for romantasy's bestsellers — a book genre that continues to explode.
item.content:encoded:
From Rhysand in A Court of Thorns and Roses to Kingfisher in "Fae and Alchemy," Anthony Palmini is a voiceover star for romantasy's bestsellers — a book genre that continues to explode.

(Image credit: Keren Carrion)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
item.description: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.
item.content:encoded:
Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.

(Image credit: Arterra)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Matt Wagner of Danish Maid Butter on the art and tradition of Easter butter lambs
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea talks with Matt Wagner, co-owner of Danish Maid Butter in Chicago, about the little Easter lambs made of butter that sell around the country this time of year.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea talks with Matt Wagner, co-owner of Danish Maid Butter in Chicago, about the little Easter lambs made of butter that sell around the country this time of year.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: An exhibit in New York examines master painter Raphael in the U.S. for the first time
item.description: For the first time in the United States, there's a major retrospective of the work of the Renaissance master Raphael.
item.content:encoded: For the first time in the United States, there's a major retrospective of the work of the Renaissance master Raphael.

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Vowel Renewals
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea plays the puzzle with KXJZ radio listener Suzanne Palmer and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea plays the puzzle with KXJZ radio listener Suzanne Palmer and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for March 28, 2026: Live in Savannah with D.W. Moffett
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Savannah with host Peter Sagal, guest Judge and Scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest D.W. Moffett and panelists Adam Burke, Shantira Jackson, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Savannah with host Peter Sagal, guest Judge and Scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest D.W. Moffett and panelists Adam Burke, Shantira Jackson, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson

(Image credit: Vivien Killilea)

item.title: 'The Comeback' is back. That's something to Cherish
item.description: The third (and final) installment of this Hollywood satire finds C-lister Valerie Cherish (Lisa Kudrow) helming an AI-written show.
item.content:encoded:
The third (and final) installment of this Hollywood satire finds C-lister Valerie Cherish (Lisa Kudrow) helming an AI-written show.

(Image credit: Erin Simkin)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon
